author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
Again on the words of the Gospel, Matt. xvii., where Jesus showed Himself on the mount to His three disciples.

title: Again on the words of the Gospel, Matt. xvii., where Jesus showed Himself on the mount to His three disciples.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We heard when the Holy Gospel was being read of the great vision on the mount, in which Jesus showed Himself to the three disciples, Peter, James, and John.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “His face did shine as the sun:” this is a figure of the shining of the Gospel.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “His raiment was white as the snow:” this is a figure of the purity of the Church, to which it was said by the Prophet, “Though your sins be as scarlet, I will make them white as snow.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Elias and Moses were talking with Him; because the grace of the Gospel receives witness from the Law and the Prophets.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Law is represented in Moses, the Prophets in Elias; to speak briefly.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For there are the mercies of God vouchsafed through a holy Martyr to be rehearsed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Peter desired three tabernacles to be made, one for Moses, one for Elias, and one for Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The solitude of the mountain had charms for him; he had been wearied with the tumult of the world’s business.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But why sought he three tabernacles, but because he knew not as yet the unity of the Law, and of Prophecy, and of the Gospel?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lastly, he was corrected by the cloud, “While he yet spake, behold, a bright cloud overshadowed them.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, the cloud hath made one tabernacle; wherefore didst thou seek for three?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “And a voice came out of the cloud, This is My beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased, hear ye Him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Elias speaketh; but “hear Him;” “Moses speaketh; but “hear Him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Prophets speak, the Law speaketh; but “hear Him,” who is the voice of the Law, and the tongue of the Prophets.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He spake in them, and when He vouchsafed so to do, He appeared in His own person.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Hear ye Him:” let us then hear Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When the Gospel spake, think it was the cloud: from thence hath the voice sounded out to us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us hear Him; that is, let us do what He saith, let us hope for what He hath promised.
